error_msg:
Json Decode Error when decoding: {
    "reasoning": "1. **Interface Generation**: The golden testbench instantiates `TopModule` as `TopModule top_module1 ( .clk, .reset, .q(q_dut) );`. This dictates that the interface must have `clk` (input), `reset` (input), and `q` (output, 4 bits). The interface is derived directly from the golden testbench's usage, following the rule to prioritize the golden testbench.\n\n2. **Testbench Improvement & Refinement**: The golden testbench was heavily modified to incorporate the new strict reporting requirements:\n    a. **Final Reporting**: The `final` block was rewritten to check `stats1.errors`. If zero, it displays \"SIMULATION PASSED\". If non-zero, it displays the required failure format: \"SIMULATION FAILED - x MISMATCHES DETECTED, FIRST AT TIME y\".\n    b. **First Mismatch Display**: The logic inside the `always @(posedge clk, negedge clk)` block was enhanced. When `stats1.errors` or `stats1.errors_q` first increments (i.e., they were 0 before the increment), state capture registers (`q_mismatch_dut_capture`, etc.) are updated, and detailed `$display` calls are made showing inputs (`clk`, `reset`), DUT output (`q_dut`), and Expected output (`q_ref`), formatted in Decimal, HEX, and Binary.\n    c. **Signal Formatting**: A `display_signal` task was introduced to handle the required formatting (Decimal, HEX, Binary) for multi-bit signals, satisfying the condition for widths <= 64.\n    d. **Error Logic Preservation**: The core error counting mechanism (`stats1.errors` and `stats1.errors_q`) remains untouched, ensuring the original functionality is maintained.\n\n3. **Syntax Advice**: The previous simulation failed due to syntax errors (e.g., incorrect system task usage, malformed procedural assignments). The generated code strictly adheres to SystemVerilog syntax. I ensured that assignments within `always` blocks use non-blocking (`<=`) where appropriate for sequential logic, and that all procedural tasks/blocks are correctly delimited. The initialization of state capture registers to 'bx' ensures that the first time they are captured reflects a valid transition.\n\n4. **Completeness**: The entire testbench, including the necessary definitions for `stimulus_gen`, `RefModule`, and the enhanced `tb` module, is provided completely.",
    "interface": "module TopModule(\n    input  logic clk,\n    input  logic reset,\n    output logic [3:0] q\n);",
    "testbench": "`timescale 1 ps/1 ps\n`define OK 12\n`define INCORRECT 13\n\n// --- Stimulus Generator (Kept as per Golden Testbench) ---\nmodule stimulus_gen (\n\tinput clk,\n\toutput reg reset,\n\toutput reg[511:0] wavedrom_title,\n\toutput reg wavedrom_enable,\n\tinput tb_match\n);\n\n\ttask reset_test(input async=0);\n\t\tbit arfail, srfail, datafail;\n\t\t\n\t\t@(posedge clk);\n\t\t@(posedge clk) reset <= 0;\n\t\trepeat(3) @(posedge clk);\n\t\t\n\t\t@(negedge clk) begin datafail = !tb_match ; reset <= 1; end\n\t\t@(posedge clk) arfail = !tb_match;\n\t\t@(posedge clk) begin\n\t\tsrfail = !tb_match;\n\t\treset <= 0;\n\t\tend\n\t\tif (srfail)\n\t\ts$display(\"Hint: Your reset doesn't seem to be working.\");\n\t\telse if (arfail && (async || !datafail))\n\t\ts$display(\"Hint: Your reset should be %0s, but doesn't appear to be.\", async ? \"asynchronous\" : \"synchronous\");\n\t\t// Don't warn about synchronous reset if the half-cycle before is already wrong. It's more likely\n\t\t// a functionality error than the reset being implemented asynchronously.\n\tendtask\n\n\t// Add two ports to module stimulus_gen:\n\t//    output [511:0] wavedrom_title\n\t//    output reg wavedrom_enable\n\n\ttask wavedrom_start(input[511:0] title = \"\");\n\tendtask\n\t\n\ttask wavedrom_stop;\n\t\t#1;\n\tendtask\n\t\t\n\t\t\n\t\tinitial begin\n\t\t\treset <= 1;\n\t\t\twavedrom_start(\"Synchronous reset and counting.\");\n\t\t\treset_test();\n\t\t\trepeat(12) @(posedge clk);\n\t\t\twavedrom_stop();\n\t\t\t@(posedge clk);\n\t\t\t\n\t\t\trepeat(400) @(posedge clk, negedge clk) begin\n\t\t\treset <= !($random & 31);\n\t\t\tend\n\t\t\t#1 $finish;\n\t\tend\n\t\tendmodule\n\n// --- DUT Implementation (Based on specification) ---\nmodule TopModule(\n    input  logic clk,\n    input  logic reset,\n    output logic [3:0] q\n);\n\n    // Internal register to hold the counter value\n    logic [3:0] counter_reg;\n\n    // Initialize the register to a known value (e.g., 0) before reset is asserted\n    initial begin\n        counter_reg = 4'b0000;\n    end\n\n    // Synchronous Decade Counter Logic (Counts 1 to 10, loops 1 -> 2 -> ... -> 10 -> 1)\n    always @(posedge clk)\n    begin\n        if (reset)\n        begin\n            // Synchronous reset sets the counter to 1\n            counter_reg <= 4'b0001;\n        end\n        else\n        begin\n            // Check for the wrap-around condition (10 -> 1)\n            if (counter_reg == 4'd10) begin\n                counter_reg <= 4'b0001; // Wrap back to 1\n            end\n            // Normal increment\n            else begin\n                counter_reg <= counter_reg + 1'b1;\n            end\n        end\n    end\n\n    // Assign the internal register to the output port\n    assign q = counter_reg;\n\nendmodule\n\n// --- Reference Module (Must exist for TB to compile) ---\nmodule RefModule (\n    input clk,\n    input reset,\n    output logic [3:0] q\n);\n    // Reference implementation (same logic)\n    reg [3:0] counter = 4'd1;\n    always @(posedge clk)\n    begin\n        if (reset)\n        begin\n            counter <= 4'd1;\n        end\n        else if (counter == 4'd10)\n        begin\n            counter <= 4'd1;\n        end\n        else\n        begin\n            counter <= counter + 1'b1;\n        end\n    end\n    assign q = counter;\nendmodule\n\n// --- Testbench ---\nmodule tb();\n\n    typedef struct packed {\n        int errors;          // Total mismatches\n        int errortime;       // Time of first mismatch\n        int errors_q;        // Mismatches specific to q output\n        int errortime_q;     // Time of first q mismatch\n\n        // State capture for detailed failure reporting\n        logic [3:0] mismatch_q_dut_capture; \n        logic [3:0] mismatch_q_ref_capture; \n        logic [3:0] mismatch_q_expected_capture; \n        int clocks;\n    } stats;\n    \n    stats stats1;\n    \n    \n    wire[511:0] wavedrom_title;\n    wire wavedrom_enable;\n    int wavedrom_hide_after_time;\n    \n    reg clk=0;\n    initial forever\n        #5 clk = ~clk;\n\n    logic reset;\n    logic [3:0] q_ref;\n    logic [3:0] q_dut;\n\n    // Signals to capture state at first error (initialized to unknown)\n    logic [3:0] q_mismatch_dut_capture = 4'bx;\n    logic [3:0] q_mismatch_ref_capture = 4'bx;\n    logic [3:0] q_mismatch_expected_capture = 4'bx;\n\n    initial begin \n        $dumpfile(\"wave.vcd\");\n        // Dump all necessary signals including captured error states\n        $dumpvars(1, stimulus_gen::stim1, tb, clk, reset, q_ref, q_dut, q_mismatch_dut_capture, q_mismatch_ref_capture, q_mismatch_expected_capture);\n    end\n\n    \n    wire tb_match;\n    wire tb_mismatch = ~tb_match;\n    \n    // Instantiate stimulus_gen (Must match golden TB instantiation structure)\n    stimulus_gen stim1 (\n        .clk, \n        .reset, \n        .wavedrom_title(wavedrom_title), \n        .wavedrom_enable(wavedrom_enable),\n        .tb_match(tb_match)\n    );\n    \n    // Instantiate RefModule\n    RefModule good1 (\n        .clk,\n        .reset,\n        .q(q_ref) );\n        \n    // Instantiate DUT\n    TopModule top_module1 (\n        .clk,\n        .reset,\n        .q(q_dut) );\n\n    \n    bit strobe = 0;\n    task wait_for_end_of_timestep;\n        repeat(5) begin\n            strobe <= !strobe;  // Try to delay until the very end of the time step.\n            @(strobe);\n        end\n    endtask\n\n    // Custom display task for multi-bit signals (Width <= 64 requirement)\n    task display_signal(input string name, input logic [3:0] signal);\n        $display(\"  [%s] Current Value: %0d (HEX: %h, BIN: %b)\", name, signal, signal, signal);\n    endtask\n    \n    final begin\n        $display(\"======================================================"

  File "/arf/scratch/merdal/mage_bench/repo/src/mage/agent.py", line 311, in _run
    self.run_instance(spec)
  File "/arf/scratch/merdal/mage_bench/repo/src/mage/agent.py", line 182, in run_instance
    testbench, _ = self.tb_gen.chat(spec)
  File "/arf/scratch/merdal/mage_bench/repo/src/mage/tb_generator.py", line 310, in chat
    raise ValueError(
